headline: AI goes agentic & IBM seals biggest deal in weeks—Claude hits 1M tokens, regulators vs. innovation, and enterprise adoption hits inflection point

stories.title: IBM closes $11B Confluent acquisition, doubles down on data streaming for enterprise AI
stories.summary: IBM completed its acquisition of Confluent on March 17 for $31/share, bringing real-time data streaming to watsonx and enabling enterprises to feed live, governed data directly into AI models and agentic systems. This is tbh massive for hybrid cloud plays.
stories.takeaway: Enterprise AI moves from pilots to production when data actually flows at scale. Incumbents are getting serious about the fundamentals others skip.

stories.title: Claude 4.6 hits 1M token context—paste entire business plans into one prompt
stories.summary: Anthropic quietly shipped Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 supporting 1M token context (roughly 750k words) at standard pricing. Game changer for consultants, analysts, and anyone doing deep document analysis.
stories.takeaway: Context window is becoming the new moat. Being able to reference an entire codebase or financial dataset in a single prompt changes how people actually work with AI.

stories.title: QuantumBlack hits production with agentic workflows—90%+ code accuracy, reshaping enterprise ops
stories.summary: McKinsey's AI arm is deploying agentic systems that handle complex end-to-end operations with 90%+ accuracy, from coding architecture to generating production-ready software. They're moving from experimentation to scalable agent factories.
stories.takeaway: Agentic AI moves from hype to measurable business impact. The firms shipping this stuff now will own entire workflow categories by year-end.

stories.title: McKinsey: adoption challenges aren't technical, they're human—orgs struggle to scale despite having tools
stories.summary: McKinsey QuantumBlack (March 11) calls out the real blocker: almost all orgs use AI, but most are stuck in early pilots because they refuse to redesign workflows. The gap between AI natives and legacy orgs is literally widening every quarter now.
stories.takeaway: You can have the best models ever. If your org structure and processes stay the same, you get 3% upside and expensive experimentation. Workflow redesign is the actual multiplier.

watchNext.title: GPT-5 shipping in April with native multimodal reasoning (text, images, audio, video in one model)
watchNext.why: This is fr the inflection moment everyone's been waiting for. If OpenAI actually nails true multimodal reasoning at GPT-4 pricing, everything changes in content, creative, and knowledge work workflows.

watchNext.title: US AI Accountability Act passes March 2026—now everyone deploying AI in hiring, lending, healthcare needs bias audits
watchNext.why: Lowkey the biggest regulation drop since the EU AI Act. This ends voluntary self-regulation and creates real compliance burden. Watch which companies get audit-ready first and which try to skirt it.
